codeReview.summary: Implementation is clean and correct. The HIP kernel correctly eliminates host round-trips by using BAR1 P2P pointers to read peer GPU partials directly in-kernel. FP32 accumulation is used as required on gfx906. The Python integration in P2PAllreduce and TPInferenceEngine follows existing project conventions. Tests pass on real hardware with max_abs_error=0.0 and 1.50x latency speedup. One non-blocking concern: there are two nearly-identical implementations (kernel_p2p_allreduce.hip vs gemv_p2p_reduce.hip/FusedP2PReduce), which the worker already noted as a pre-existing issue.

issues.description: The odd-element scalar path at `else if (idx < n)` only fires for the very last element when n is odd. Since hidden_size=5120 in Qwen 3.5 27B (always even), this is never exercised in practice. Not a bug, but dead code for the current model. No issue for production.

allreduce_residual_kernel() calls hip.synchronize() (device-sync) on ALL GPUs before launching kernels (lines 291-293). This is overly conservative — the non-blocking allreduce streams and event-based synchronization used by the C dispatch path handle this more efficiently. For the Python path (non-C-dispatch), this sync prevents overlap and adds host overhead. Could use event-based sync instead, but this is performance-only and the Python path is not the primary hot path.

The try/except around _load_kernel_p2p_lib() silently ignores all exceptions (`pass`). This means a failed build (e.g., hipcc not found, compilation error) will silently leave _kernel_p2p_lib=None. Only a print warning is shown if the lib is unavailable. Adding a debug log of the exception would aid diagnosis.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Kernel P2P Allreduce section in .factory/library/architecture.md was added by the worker and is comprehensive. It documents the performance numbers, design decisions, and integration details correctly.
No new commands were needed in services.yaml for this feature. The existing build_all and test_tp4 commands suffice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature only uses hipcc (via _load_kernel_p2p_lib auto-build) and existing test runner

summary: PASS. Kernel P2P allreduce implementation is correct and complete. VAL-KP2P-001 (max_abs_error < 1e-3, achieved: 0.0) and VAL-KP2P-003 (latency < 119us, achieved: 79.2us = 1.50x speedup) are both satisfied. The kernel design is appropriate for gfx906/PCIe (FP32 accumulation, half2 vectorization, correct grid sizing). The Python integration follows project conventions. Non-blocking issues noted are informational only.
